$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the privileges the synthwatch-api managed identity (MI) requires.
Motivated by four separate missing-grant root-causes (dismiss-500, on-demand reconcile, spec_cache,
AOAI/email). The grants split across TWO planes:
• azureRbac  — Azure control/data-plane role assignments. ENFORCED in CI: scripts/check-grant-coverage.mjs
asserts this set EXACTLY matches the role assignments in infra/main.bicep (fail-closed).
• postgres   — table grants on the MI's Postgres role. Granted by RUNNER-owned migrations (separate repo),
so they're DOCUMENTED here (the single place that lists everything) but NOT CI-enforced from
this repo — asserting them needs the live DB / the runner's migrations.
To add an endpoint that needs a new Azure role: add it to azureRbac AND grant it in main.bicep — the CI
check fails until both are present (a new endpoint can't merge without its grant).
principal: synthwatch-api Function App system-assigned managed identity

azureRbac.reason: Functions host: deployment package (app-package container) + AzureWebJobs host state (shared-key disabled).
Functions host: AzureWebJobs queue state on the deployment storage account.
Read runner-owned trace/screenshot blobs to stream them through the proxy endpoints.
Microsoft.App/jobs/start/action — start the runner job on-demand ('Run now' / test-send).
Microsoft.App/jobs/start/action — start the reconcile job on-demand.
Data-plane chat-completions on the AOAI account via DefaultAzureCredential (the RCA / why-is-this-failing path).
Send OTP sign-in / edit-access emails through ACS via DefaultAzureCredential.

$comment: ★ NOW CI-ENFORCED (scripts/check-pg-grant-coverage.mjs) — this closes the gap the old comment named.
The two worst 500s this session lived on this plane: dismiss-500 (missing DELETE on access_requests),
approve-500 (missing UPDATE on reconcile_apply_plan). They are INVISIBLE to admin psql + Testcontainers
(both run as SUPERUSER, and the snapshot has no synthwatch-api role) — they only surface as a prod 500.
The check compares:
REQUIRED  = `writes` below (the WRITE privileges the synthwatch-api role needs, per the handlers).
GRANTED   = the GRANT … TO \"synthwatch-api\" statements parsed from the RUNNER repo's db/migrations/*.sql
(the runner owns the schema/grants), UNIONed with `opsBaseline` below.
It FAILS if any required (table, privilege) is in neither — naming the table + missing privilege.
Why an allowlist (approach b), not parsing the C#: the write surface is part raw SQL (regex-able) and
part EF change-tracking — incl. IMPLICIT updates (a fetched-then-mutated entity + SaveChanges emits
UPDATE with no syntactic marker, e.g. PATCH check / edit channel). That makes UPDATEs unreliable to
enumerate statically, so the required WRITE set is a maintained allowlist. (The check ALSO scans the
API's raw-SQL writes and fails if one names a table absent from `writes` — an auto-catch for the common
case.) SELECT is covered for every table by ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES (ops), so only WRITES are listed.
TO ADD an endpoint that writes a NEW table/privilege: add it to `writes` here AND add a runner migration
`GRANT <priv> ON <table> TO \"synthwatch-api\";` — CI fails until both are present (no merge without the grant).

writes.neededBy: the authz/audit envelope writes one row per authorized mutation
POST/PUT/DELETE /api/env-domain-map (env PR-3 map management page). GRANT in runner migration 0075.
create / PATCH / delete check; reconcile-apply materialize

$comment: WRITE grants applied at role setup (Azure ops), NOT via a repo migration — so the migration parse
can't see them and would false-fail without this list. Verified against the live DB on 2026-07-01:
SELECT table_name, string_agg(privilege_type,',') FROM information_schema.role_table_grants
WHERE grantee='synthwatch-api' GROUP BY 1;
`checks` is the only API-write table granted out-of-band (every other write table is migration-granted).
Re-verify + update this if the bootstrap grants ever change.
